author: conductor
title: Retrieval-first enforcement — knowledge delivery + mechanized staging (front half of the learning loop)
problem: The retrieval-first rule is prose and gets skipped under momentum. Trigger (SpaceExplorer/Comsoft conductor-direct session, 2026-07-19, user verbatim): the conductor 'repeatedly started work — code edits, and even a requirements grill — by reading CODE first and staging knowledge articles late or not at all. I had to call it out three separate times ("you didn't read any articles?", "are the hooks not forcing you to read the kb?")'. Clarified (user verbatim): 'the comsoft Claude only read the code as preparation as part of knowledge retrieval. it didnt take the articles related so it ended up suggestion a bad solution.' The learning loop read-the-store -> act -> capture -> reconcile is enforced only on the back half (H7/H10/H15); the front half has no mechanism.
feature: Per decision fe62546f: knowledge delivery + mechanized staging, never blocking. File-touch injection of the owning-article payload per the probe-gated fallback ladder (rung 1 first-Read injection -> rung 2 first-Edit/Write injection -> rung 3 UserPromptSubmit/territory-entry staging only); payload = owning article substance + one-hop pointers; frontier signal on unowned governed touches; session delivery guard with per-file re-arm; dispatch staging for declared file_keys; pipeline unchanged (prep.mjs); grill-entry protection = mechanized staging at grill-skill entry (supersedes the earlier grill-entry deny-gate answer). Concept family: knowledge-delivery (article 49722544).

criteria: Verbatim: 'The retrieval-first rule is prose, so under momentum it gets skipped' — the front half must get teeth like the back half (H7/H10/H15) has.
Verbatim: 'Re-arming: when work grows into NEW territory mid-session, the demand must fire again (today's failure was exactly scope growth without re-staging).'
Verbatim: 'P1 check: the gate must change outcomes, not add ceremony.'
Chosen (fork): 'Deliver + mechanize' — no blocking gate for now; toothed gate revisited as its own later decision only if skip-failures recur.
Chosen (frontier limitation): 'Solve — add a frontier signal' — loud unowned-territory notice on governed touches with no owning article.
Chosen (ladder): 'Approve the ladder' — probe at build start decides rung 1/2/3.
Chosen (split): jargon/glossary grill rule split out as its own item (board ef157057).
constraints: One-hop knowledge reach preserved (user's original evidence answer 'owning article + one hop' carried into the payload as pointers).
Session-scoped delivery state, whole session, no expiry (user's TTL answer); re-arm via per-file keying.
Dispatch coverage keyed on structurally declared file_keys only — no free-text prompt parsing (user's dispatch answer, P3).
Pipeline phases keep prep.mjs staging — no double-delivery inside runs.

conductor_proposals.text: Fallback ladder (rung 1 Read-injection -> rung 2 Edit-injection -> rung 3 staging-only) with a live probe at build start deciding the rung; build records which rung was proven and why.
Payload shape: owning article substance + one-hop slug pointers instead of full one-hop bodies (bounded injection; no platform dedup exists).
Session delivery guard (H13-style ledger) required because injections accumulate verbatim with no platform de-duplication (research_finding b7d4aeac).
Grill-entry deny-gate replaced by mechanized staging at grill-skill entry — supersession of the earlier grill-entry staging-gate answer, confirmed with AC set.
MCP hook observability contradiction (docs vs recorded 2.1.198 finding) tracked as probe-and-revisit board item 00845d07, out of scope here.
This machine's empty local KB (store content lives on the dev machine) undermines every retrieval mechanism including this one — recommend a separate sync/populate task. Raised, not yet accepted.

acceptance_criteria.text: Probe first: a captured research_finding for the exact Claude Code version in use records which ladder rung was proven, before any rung-specific code merges.

Delivery fires: in a conductor-direct session, first touch (Read on rung 1 / Edit-Write on rung 2) of a file owned by a feature_article puts the payload (owning substance + one-hop pointers) into the conductor's context with zero conductor action, visible in the transcript. On rung 3 the same payload is staged mechanically at territory entry (UserPromptSubmit), including grill-skill entry.
Frontier signal: the same touch on a governed file with NO owning article delivers the loud 'territory unowned — H10 will demand an article' notice.

Guarded, re-arming: repeat touches of already-delivered territory do not re-inject within a session; a first touch of NEW territory mid-session always delivers. Guard state is session-lifecycle-bound (P4).
Dispatch staging: a subagent dispatch with declared file_keys gets the payload staged into its brief/handoff mechanically; undeclared dispatches are unchanged.
Pipeline untouched: no double-delivery inside runs — prep.mjs staging remains the pipeline path.
Never blocks: no tool call is ever denied by this mechanism.

technical_design.approach: Intake-level only — planning owns the design. Settled intent: probe-gated fallback ladder; reuse H7's file->owning-article join, H2's additionalContext primitive, H13/lib-ledger machinery for the guard, prep.mjs pattern for staging. See decision fe62546f and concept article 49722544.

out_of_scope: Any blocking gate, including the server-side delivered-evidence ledger — deferred to its own future decision, triggered only if skip-failures recur with delivery in place.
The jargon/glossary grill rule — split out by user decision (board ef157057).
Revisiting the H11 architecture or adopting hook-side MCP observation — probe-and-revisit board item 00845d07.
Pipeline staging changes beyond the no-double-delivery guarantee — prep.mjs stays as is.
Assistant-prose enforcement (grill questions, jargon hard-stops) — no platform surface exists; stays SOP discipline.
This machine's empty-KB problem (store sync from the dev machine) — standing issue, separate from the mechanism.
